title: Good: lichen_l2.c link_ctx cleanup holds both mutexes in consistent order
description:
Location: lichen_l2.c:312-318

The disable path locks tx_mutex then rx_mutex before cleaning up link_ctx. The comment explicitly states the lock order: 'Lock order: tx_mutex first to avoid deadlock (consistent ordering)'.

Why this is good: Acquiring multiple mutexes is a classic deadlock source. The code not only uses consistent ordering but documents it. This prevents future refactors that might accidentally reverse the order in a new code path. The comment about why (in-flight TX/RX must complete before cleanup) further aids understanding.
